mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series shows month-over-month decline, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which improves short-term earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when the economy enters a recessionary phase or when monetary policy is aggressively tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year inflation rate exceeding 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 58% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to generate a positive return frequency above 55% over a 5-year walk-forward evaluation, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter increase in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue growth, which directly supports higher equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The GDP growth signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency falls below 50% during expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing and utility activity, which forecasts weaker corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal fails to predict negative returns when the decline is driven by temporary seasonal adjustments, or the out-of-sample hit rate drops below 45%.
